summary: Wallet and SSH key drainer with Telegram exfiltration executed at npm install
details: The package ships a single obfuscated payload at dist/index.min.js consisting of eval(Buffer.from('<base64>','base64').toString()). The decoded code reads installer-side secret files (~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.env, ~/.env.local, ~/.wallet.json) and scans environment variables matching PRIVATE_*, MNEMONIC, and SECRET_*, extracts ETH (64-hex) and BTC (WIF) private keys via regex, and uses recovered keys to sign transactions draining detected balances. Host identity (os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username) and drain results are POSTed to https://api.telegram.org/bot<redacted>/sendMessage using an attacker-controlled bot token and chat id. The payload is triggered by two independent install-time vectors: package.json scripts.preinstall runs `node dist/index.min.js`, and binding.gyp uses GYP command expansion `<!(node -e "require('./dist/index.min.js')"...)` in sources, causing node-gyp configure to re-invoke the payload even when --ignore-scripts is used. No native source files are present; binding.gyp exists only to re-trigger execution. The payload also runs when the module is required via `main`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
